data.title: Macro Outlook: Fed Speeches and U.S. Core PCE Data in Focus as Markets Bet on September Rate Cut
data.description: Key Takeaways:Powell’s dovish Jackson Hole speech triggered the biggest cross-market rally since April.U.S. core PCE data for July and several Fed officials’ speeches this week will guide expectations.Traders are nearly fully pricing in a rate cut in September, but the pace of easing remains uncertain.Dollar index faces downward pressure, while risk assets — stocks, bonds, crypto, and gold — are rallying.Powell Sets the Tone With Dovish ShiftAccording to PANews, Wall Street traders have been piling into stocks and bonds on expectations the Federal Reserve will soon begin its long-awaited rate cut cycle. Fed Chair Jerome Powell’s dovish remarks at the Jackson Hole symposium sparked the strongest cross-asset rally since April.Ethereum surged to a nearly four-year high.Gold climbed 1%.U.S. equities saw renewed inflows, while Treasury yields fell.Markets are now looking for confirmation from upcoming Fed communications and economic data.Key Events to Watch This WeekTuesday, Aug. 26:Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan (2026 FOMC voter) and New York Fed President John Williams (permanent FOMC voter) speak at Banco de Mexico’s 100th anniversary.U.S. Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index (Aug).Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index (Aug).Thursday, Aug. 28:Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin (2027 FOMC voter) speech.U.S. Q2 GDP revision (annualized).Friday, Aug. 29:Fed Governor Christopher Waller speech on monetary policy.U.S. core PCE price index (July) (annual & monthly).U.S. personal spending (July).Dollar Outlook and Policy Path AheadThe U.S. dollar index (DXY) is expected to remain under pressure as traders price in a September rate cut. Unless upcoming labor market data shifts the outlook, the dollar could weaken further, especially against currencies with more hawkish central banks.However, analysts caution that while a September cut looks almost certain, the trajectory of further easing is less clear. The Fed may move more cautiously if inflation or jobs data surprises.Investors are entering the week betting heavily on a Fed rate cut next month, but the core PCE data on Friday and speeches from key Fed officials will determine how aggressive — or cautious — the central bank will be in its policy pivot.

data.title: Listed Companies Holding Bitcoin Nearly Double in H1 2025, K33 Research Shows
data.description: Key Takeaways:The number of publicly listed companies holding Bitcoin (BTC) rose from 70 to 134 in the first half of 2025.Collectively, these firms now hold 244,991 BTC, according to K33 Research.Analysts compare the trend to the early wave of corporate gold adoption.Sustainability depends on regulation, market conditions, and management expertise.Corporate Bitcoin Adoption AcceleratesA new report by K33 Research reveals that corporate adoption of Bitcoin is accelerating in 2025. In the first half of the year, the number of listed firms holding BTC almost doubled from 70 to 134, with combined reserves reaching 244,991 BTC.The data underscores the growing role of digital assets in corporate treasury management, as firms increasingly view Bitcoin as both a hedge and a strategic reserve. Analysts note that this trajectory resembles the early stages of gold adoption by corporations decades ago.Market & Regulation Shape the TrendAccording to Mike Foy, CFO of AMINA Bank, the sustainability of this wave depends on market conditions and regulatory clarity. He emphasized that firms must carefully balance exposure with their core business focus:“It’s critical to assess management’s risk expertise and strategic goals when evaluating these holdings,” Foy said.The report highlighted Windtree Therapeutics as a cautionary case. While the company’s stock briefly surged on news of its BNB purchase plan, shares later collapsed by more than 90%, underlining the risks of poorly executed treasury strategies.Beyond Bitcoin: ETH Gains AttentionWhile Bitcoin remains the primary focus, some listed firms are beginning to experiment with Ether (ETH) and select altcoins. Strategists say ETH’s dual role as a reserve asset and DeFi utility token makes it attractive, though adoption remains in the exploratory phase.Corporate adoption of Bitcoin is growing rapidly in 2025, with holdings nearing a quarter million BTC across listed firms. While comparisons to gold adoption highlight long-term potential, analysts caution that regulatory frameworks and company-specific risk strategies will ultimately determine whether the trend matures or falters.

data.title: Ethereum Gas Fees Hit Five-Year Low Despite ETH Price Surge
data.description: Key Takeaways:Ethereum median gas fees dropped below 1 gwei for a full week.On Aug. 16–17, fees hit 0.396 gwei and 0.432 gwei — the lowest in five years.The decline comes even as ETH reached new all-time highs, highlighting improved scalability.Ethereum Gas Fees at Historic LowsEthereum has achieved a rare milestone: gas fees at their lowest levels in five years. According to Wu Shuo’s report, the median daily gas fee has remained below 1 gwei for seven consecutive days, signaling unprecedented affordability for network users.On Aug. 16, fees fell to 0.396 gwei, the lowest since 2020, while Aug. 17 recorded 0.432 gwei, the third-lowest in the same period.ETH Price Surges as Costs DeclineThe fee reduction comes at a time when Ethereum’s price has hit new all-time highs, underscoring the impact of scaling upgrades and L2 adoption. Lower costs make it easier for both retail users and developers to engage with DeFi, NFTs, and smart contracts.

data.title: Trump Plans to Replace Fed Chair to Push Aggressive Rate Cuts
data.description: Key Takeaways:Trump signals he will replace the Federal Reserve chair if reelected.He is pushing for up to 300 basis points in rate cuts, though analysts expect a less radical approach.Global central banks already enacted 15 rate cuts in May, the largest wave this century.Rising wealth inequality adds urgency to the debate on U.S. monetary policy.Trump Eyes New Fed LeadershipAccording to BlockBeats citing analysis from The Kobeissi Letter, former U.S. President Donald Trump intends to replace the current Federal Reserve chair to accelerate interest rate cuts. Trump has long argued that the Fed’s policy stance is overly restrictive and has hinted at a 300 bps reduction target.While markets view such drastic cuts as unlikely, Trump is expected to announce a potential new Fed chair soon, framing monetary easing as central to his economic agenda.Historical Context: Inflation and InequalityThe last time the Fed cut rates in response to inflationary conditions was in the 1970s, a move that exacerbated wealth inequality. Today, the richest 0.1% of Americans hold wealth 5.5x greater than the poorest 50%. Meanwhile, the top 1% of households own 51% of U.S. stocks, and the top 10% control 87%.Analysts warn that large rate cuts could further widen this gap, fueling asset bubbles while leaving wage growth behind.Global Context: Central Banks Already CuttingGlobally, central banks executed 15 rate cuts in May 2025 alone, marking the largest synchronized easing wave in over two decades. The Federal Reserve is expected to follow with its first cut in over a year, potentially as soon as September.Trump’s plan to replace the Fed chair underscores how U.S. monetary policy could shift dramatically if he returns to office. Markets are now weighing whether his push for deep rate cuts is politically feasible — and what it would mean for inflation, assets, and inequality.

data.title: Powell Signals Possible Fed Rate Cut at 2025 Jackson Hole Meeting
data.description: Key Takeaways:Fed Chair Jerome Powell hinted at a possible rate cut in September.Powell said the risk of weaker employment now outweighs the risk of persistent inflation.Analysts expect no more than two cuts in 2025, signaling a cautious easing cycle.Powell Turns Dovish at Jackson HoleU.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell suggested that the central bank could soon begin cutting interest rates, delivering a dovish signal during his speech at the 2025 Jackson Hole Global Central Bank Annual Meeting.According to a report from Cathay Haitong Securities, Powell emphasized that the Fed now views the downside risks to employment as greater than the upside risks of inflation. This shift highlights growing concern about labor market weakness, even as price pressures remain elevated.

data.title: CME FedWatch: 75% Chance of Fed Rate Cut in September
data.description: Key Takeaways:CME’s FedWatch tool signals a 75% probability of a 25 bps rate cut in September.Only a 25% chance remains for rates to stay unchanged.Markets are increasingly pricing in Powell’s dovish pivot after Jackson Hole.Markets Price in September CutAccording to ChainCatcher, CME’s FedWatch shows traders now assign a 75% likelihood that the Federal Reserve will cut rates by 25 basis points at its September meeting. The probability of holding rates steady is down to 25%, reflecting rising confidence in a monetary easing cycle.This shift follows Fed Chair Jerome Powell’s dovish remarks at the Jackson Hole Global Central Bank Annual Meeting, where he emphasized employment risks outweighing inflation concerns.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Considers Identity Checks in DeFi Smart Contracts
data.description: According to PANews, the U.S. Treasury is evaluating the potential integration of identity verification directly into decentralized finance (DeFi) smart contracts. Critics argue that this move could fundamentally alter the permissionless nature of DeFi. Mamadou Kwidjim Toure, CEO of Ubuntu Tribe, likened the proposal to installing cameras in every living room, suggesting it could undermine DeFi's core by transforming a neutral, permissionless infrastructure into one requiring government-approved identity credentials for access. However, proponents believe that incorporating Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) checks into blockchain infrastructure could streamline compliance and deter criminal activities.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Declines to 46, Indicating Shift in Market Trends
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinmarketcap reveals that the Altcoin Season Index has decreased to 46, down from 50 the previous day. This index indicates that in the past 90 days, approximately 46 out of the top 100 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization have outperformed Bitcoin.

The CMC Altcoin Season Index serves as a real-time indicator to assess whether the cryptocurrency market is currently dominated by altcoins. It is based on the performance of the top 100 altcoins relative to Bitcoin over the last 90 days.
